steps.title: Factor the second equation
steps.content: The second equation is $y = 6x - 12$. Notice that the right side can be factored: $$6x - 12 = 6(x - 2)$$ So the system becomes: $$y = (x-2)(x+4)$$ $$y = 6(x-2)$$

steps.title: Set equations equal and factor
steps.content: Since both expressions equal $y$, set them equal: $$(x-2)(x+4) = 6(x-2)$$ Move everything to one side and factor out the common term $(x-2)$: $$(x-2)(x+4) - 6(x-2) = 0$$ $$(x-2)[(x+4) - 6] = 0$$ $$(x-2)(x - 2) = 0$$ Therefore $x = 2$.

steps.title: Substitute to find y and identify the solution
steps.content: Substitute $x = 2$ into the second equation: $$y = 6(2) - 12 = 12 - 12 = 0$$ The solution is the ordered pair $(2, 0)$. That matches choice C - C is correct.
